ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Closing The Estate
Off Calendar Nov-16-2020 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate Is Set For November 16, 2020
Off Calendar Nov-16-2020 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-21-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Avid Alavi appeared by zoom for Roy Grunow (Executor).

The Court stated that the only reason this matter is on for an appearance is because of the issue of the bank account exceeding the FDIC insured amount. Ms. Alavi stated that there is no excuse for that, that she is pretty sure she told her Client about the FDIC requirement, that her Client is elderly, not mobile and has significant medical issues, that it was very difficult for him to open one bank account and that it would have been extremely difficult for her Client to open multiple bank accounts. Ms. Alavi also stated that her Client is also the Beneficiary of those funds, that the funds are secure, that she was not aware of the issue, that she will do better with that going forward and asked the Court to allow the accounting. The Final Report of Executor on Waiver of Account, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: G. Ideta. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Aug-28-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Avid Alavi for Roy Grunow.

The Court noted that it reviewed the declarations and stated that it is satisfied that the Decedent lacked capacity at the time of death and therefore, the presumption of revocation does not attach. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Lost Will and for Letters Testamentary (subject to review of the order). Bond is not required. Status on close of estate is set for November 16, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-29-2019 Continued To Aug-26-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Avid Alavi for Roy Grunow.

Ms. Alavi stated that there was not an original duplicate of the will, that it was her error, that it's just a copy of the will that was located in the house, that the Decedent and her Client were married for 44 years and lived in the family home for 36 years and that she can present more evidence on the issue of presumption of revocation. Ms. Alavi also stated that the Husband remembers that they put the original estate documents in the house cabinet, that the Husband is unable to locate his original will either and that they tried to contact the drafting Attorney, but he has resigned and they were unable to make contact with him. In addition, Ms. Alavi stated that she had contact with a former Real Estate Agent, that there was no marital discord, that the original could be with the drafting Attorney, that they (Decedent and Husband) came to her office together, that she gave notice to all of the trust Beneficiaries and the Heirs at law, that no one is objecting and that when Ms. Grunow came to her office in October she had capacity. The Court stated that there are two issues, the issue of due diligence and the issue of capacity. Ms. Alavi spoke of the Decedent's medical condition, stated that she could speak with her Client about getting something from the Decedent's Doctors, that this is only for half of the property because it is a bequest from a Friend's trust, that the other half of the property was left to the Husband and spoke of the close relationships the Decedent and the Husband had with their neighbors/community. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to August 26, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-10-2019 Continued To Jul-29-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
